def test_type_coercion_price():
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'C', 'price': 150}
    ]
    threshold = 150
    # Expected result: only items with price > 150, so only item B
    expected = [{'name': 'B', 'price': '200'}]
    # The function should handle string prices by converting to int for comparison
    result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, but got {result}"

attempts.test_code:
from source import filter_expensive_items

def test_type_coercion_price():
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'},
        {'name': 'C', 'price': 150},
        {'name': 'D', 'price': '150'}
    ]
    threshold = 150
    
    result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)
    
    assert result == [{'name': 'B', 'price': '200'}], \
        "Should correctly filter items with price > 150, converting string prices to integers for comparison"
